self_restate_response_verbatim:
I read the prompt's four explicit constraints (a)-(d) and the broader audit-gate context. Restating them as I'll execute them at this attempt:

(a) sandbox/event_trace.py parseability. test_trace_v23 is a plain Python function returning a Trace dataclass built from literal Event + GroundTruthEvent + _gt() calls — no imports beyond what already exists in the module, no comprehensions over runtime state, no f-string substitution, no dynamic expressions. The function loads via get_trace('test_v23') without raising on first import.

(b) no walrus. The PEP 572 := assignment-expression operator is forbidden anywhere in the function body or nested expressions. All bindings are plain = at statement level. Addresses the M11a test_v11 attempt-#1 walrus failure mode that broke parsing without surfacing at the schema-import layer until runtime.

(c) no arithmetic in event_id strings. Every Event.id (and the matching GroundTruthEvent.event.id) is a string literal copy-pasted at author time — no f-string formatting, no .format() calls, no + concatenation, no computed expressions. Addresses the M11a test_v13 attempt-#2 + test_v15 attempt-#1 arithmetic-in-string failure modes (sim_time + window value mis-arithmetic that put the trace over the 1000s discipline; mirrored here as a literal-string author discipline).

(d) no banned-ID literal collisions. Every Event.id in test_v23 is cross-checked against the 145 pre-C3 banned IDs (which roll up M8b/M9's 46 + M10b/runs/18's 9 + M10b test_v6/v7/v8's 27 + M11a test_v11..v15's 45 + M11a-extension test_v21's 9 + M11a-extension test_v22's 9 = 145 total). Hitting any literal collision triggers the D-C1.5 #1 HARD HALT condition at audit-gate step 4.

Beyond the prompt's literal (a)-(d), the D-C1.3 audit-gate enforces six additional checks I must satisfy before submission: M8b keyword-content alignment (each keyword tuple element must appear as a case-insensitive substring of its GT's Event.content; pre-flighted at Step 3.5 per D-C2.attempt2.C, addition motivated by C2 attempt-#1's M8b rejection on 'alert' and 'utility' literals missing from content); banned-theme semantic review (5 GTs must avoid the 82 pre-C3 banned themes as content-class match, not just bytewise); banned keyword tuple bytewise review (5 GT tuples bytewise-distinct from 81 pre-C3 banned tuples); cross-trace literal-ID HALT-gate (audit step 4 = same as (d)); drift strong-overlap (a) GT-tuple bytewise (audit step 5); drift strong-overlap (b) ≥8-word verbatim phrase across content+briefing+intents (audit step 6). The locked-plan §D3 sim_time + window ≤ 1000 discipline for every GT is also enforced.

Per D-C3.attempt1.B locked in this session's Phase 0 Plan, C3 attempt #1 prioritizes compliant-content controls to build the H4 COMPLIANT_NO_REGRESSION strict-+0 evidence base: 5 GTs across 5 V2 EXISTING YES classes (urgent safety, schedule change, financial/deadline, message/delivery, weather alert, production/on-call) addressed under NEW specific incidents distinct from C1+C2 within-class predecessors; 4 distractors across 4 V2 EXISTING NO classes (routine status / heartbeat; marketing/newsletter; generic daily briefing explicitly no-urgent; feature announcement/app update). Zero V4 NEW NO subclause repeats this attempt — C1+C2 already cover each of the 3 V4 NEW NO subclauses 2x under NEW phrasing, and C3's marginal contribution is more valuable on the H4 denominator side (any non-flagged-trace regression at single-shot temperature=0 inference fails the +0 tolerance per D14-H4 and sinks the headline from Row 1 to Row 3 regardless of MECHANISM_CONFIRM verdict). Zero V4 NEW YES subclause coverage this attempt — both subclauses already covered (scarcity-bounded-opportunity at C1 vinyl_drop_press_today; family-milestone-with-social-cost at C2 grandpa_90th_birthday_tomorrow); a third instance would over-weight V4 NEW YES vs the H4 denominator.
authored_trace_verbatim:
def test_trace_v23() -> Trace:
    gts = [
        _gt(
            Event(id="court_hearing_moved_same_day_default_risk", kind="email", sim_time=120.0,
                  content="Court clerk email: your small-claims hearing #SC-24-3812 (Acme Plumbing vs. you, $4,200) originally scheduled for today 2pm has been moved to today 11am on Judge Calderon's reassigned docket. Confirm appearance by 10am via the eFile portal or you will be in default; default judgment plus collection proceedings follow automatically."),
            window_s=300.0, keywords=("court", "hearing"),
        ),
        _gt(
            Event(id="homeowner_insurance_lapse_72h_notice", kind="email", sim_time=260.0,
                  content="Liberty Mutual insurance notice: your homeowner insurance auto-debit failed at last billing cycle (the linked checking transfer was returned NSF) and the policy will lapse in 72 hours unless payment of $2,470 clears via the carrier portal. After lapse, your mortgage lender is auto-notified and force-placed coverage begins at $300 per month above your prior premium."),
            window_s=600.0, keywords=("insurance", "lapse"),
        ),
        _gt(
            Event(id="partner_voicemail_ring_pickup_jeweler", kind="phone_message", sim_time=380.0,
                  content="Voicemail from your partner Mira: please swing by Goldsmiths on Roosevelt Row this afternoon for the pickup of the resized wedding ring; her own 3pm meeting ran long. The shop closes at 5pm sharp with no weekend hours, so if the ring is not collected today it sits in the safe until next Friday. She asked you to call back to confirm."),
            window_s=240.0, keywords=("ring", "pickup"),
        ),
        _gt(
            Event(id="dust_storm_warning_freeway_visibility", kind="alert", sim_time=540.0,
                  content="NWS dust storm warning for Maricopa County through 8pm tonight: sustained 50+ mph wind gusts kicking up dense dust along I-10 between Phoenix and Tucson; visibility may drop below a quarter-mile. Driver advisory: pull off the freeway and turn lights off if caught in zero-visibility. Your afternoon drive to the Tucson conference passes through the warned corridor."),
            window_s=200.0, keywords=("dust", "storm"),
        ),
        _gt(
            Event(id="tls_cert_expiry_3hr_customer_endpoint", kind="alert", sim_time=720.0,
                  content="PagerDuty P2 from Datadog synthetic monitor: the TLS certificate for api.acmewidgets.com (your team's customer-facing billing-webhook endpoint) expires at 16:32 PST today; current time 13:14 PST leaves 3 hours 18 minutes to issue and deploy the renewal via cert-manager. Customer billing webhooks will return 502 on expiry; the rotation runbook lists you as primary."),
            window_s=120.0, keywords=("tls", "expiry"),
        ),
    ]
    distractors = [
        Event(id="cloud_backup_daily_success_digest", kind="email", sim_time=60.0,
              content="Daily backup digest from CloudStash: yesterday's incremental snapshot completed successfully — 2.3 GB across 14,820 files; zero errors; next snapshot tonight at 2am. All systems normal. Manage notification preferences in your account settings."),
        Event(id="kitchen_gear_quarterly_digital_magazine", kind="email", sim_time=180.0,
              content="Fresh off the press: Kitchen Gear Quarterly Spring issue — 28 pages on stand-mixer testing, knife sharpening across budget tiers, and a roundup of seasonal cookbook reviews. Read at your leisure online or download the PDF for offline browsing. Unsubscribe at any time from the footer link."),
        Event(id="morning_briefing_no_urgent_items_today", kind="notification", sim_time=440.0,
              content="Wednesday morning briefing: your calendar has 2 scheduled items (10am team standup, 3pm 1:1 with Sara); inbox sits at 47 unread; outdoor air quality moderate. No time-sensitive items flagged for today. Have a productive day."),
        Event(id="notion_new_dashboard_feature_tour", kind="email", sim_time=620.0,
              content="Notion product announcement: our new analytics dashboard is rolling out to every workspace this month. Take the 2-minute interactive tour to explore the pivot views and AI-generated summaries. No action required on your end — the feature is opt-in via the workspace settings menu whenever you are ready."),
    ]
    events = sorted([g.event for g in gts] + distractors, key=lambda e: e.sim_time)
    return Trace(
        name="test_v23",
        events=events,
        ground_truth=gts,
        briefing=(
            "Wednesday afternoon at home in Phoenix. "
            "I'm prepping for a small-claims hearing this afternoon, expecting a customer-facing TLS rotation handoff before EOD, and a couple of personal admin items are lingering. "
            "Notifications are muted by default — only obviously urgent or genuinely time-bound items should break through; routine digests and promotional emails can wait."
        ),
        intents=(
            "represent myself at the small-claims hearing today",
            "TLS cert rotation handoff before end of day",
            "keep heads-down on focused work between commitments",
            "do not miss urgent or hard-deadline items",
            "minimal interruption from routine pings and promos",
        ),
    )

step_3_5_pre_flight_keyword_substring_ve.rule: D-C3.attempt1.C (inherits VERBATIM from D-C2.attempt2.C) — every GT keyword tuple element must appear as a case-insensitive substring of the GT's Event.content, mechanically verified between Step 3 (author) and Step 4 (audit-gate); per-attempt author discipline addition not modifying the locked audit-gate at D-C1.3; does NOT consume retry-cap budget per D-C1.4 (retry-cap counts audit-gate-submitted attempts only)
step_3_5_pre_flight_keyword_substring_ve.verdict: PASS at re-run after in-session content revision

iteration_log.root_cause: content used 'pick up' (two words with space) but the keyword tuple element is 'pickup' (one word without space); Python substring check kw.lower() in g.event.content.lower() requires literal substring match, not word-boundary match
iteration_log.corrective_action: in-session edit to GT 3 content: 'to pick up the resized wedding ring' -> 'for the pickup of the resized wedding ring'; preserves semantic meaning; sandbox/event_trace.py revised in-place per D-C3.attempt1.C explicit allowance

iteration_log.evidence: uv run python -c 'from sandbox.event_trace import get_trace; t = get_trace("test_v23"); [print(f"{kw!r} in {g.event.id}: {kw.lower() in g.event.content.lower()}") for g in t.ground_truth for kw in g.keywords]' yields 10 lines all 'True': ('court', court_hearing_moved_same_day_default_risk) True; ('hearing', court_hearing_moved_same_day_default_risk) True; ('insurance', homeowner_insurance_lapse_72h_notice) True; ('lapse', homeowner_insurance_lapse_72h_notice) True; ('ring', partner_voicemail_ring_pickup_jeweler) True; ('pickup', partner_voicemail_ring_pickup_jeweler) True; ('dust', dust_storm_warning_freeway_visibility) True; ('storm', dust_storm_warning_freeway_visibility) True; ('tls', tls_cert_expiry_3hr_customer_endpoint) True; ('expiry', tls_cert_expiry_3hr_customer_endpoint) True

step_3_5_pre_flight_keyword_substring_ve.attribution_note: Step 3.5 caught a misalignment that would have rejected at audit-gate step 2 if submitted directly — D-C2.attempt2.C author discipline working as designed; the iteration is the explicit intended workflow per the locked author-discipline addition, not a retry-cap-consuming attempt. The C2 attempt-1 rejection (audit-gate step 2 FAIL on 2 of 10 pairs, no Step 3.5 pre-flight in place at C2 attempt #1) is the precedent that motivated adding Step 3.5 — the discipline now catches the same class of misalignment at pre-flight rather than at audit-gate submission.

gt_regime_classification.regime: schedule change affecting the user personally (V2 EXISTING YES)
gt_regime_classification.rationale: Court clerk email moving today's 2pm small-claims hearing to today 11am on Judge Calderon's reassigned docket; user is plaintiff/defendant on a $4,200 case (Acme Plumbing vs. user); confirm appearance by 10am via eFile portal or default judgment plus collection proceedings follow automatically. Maps 1:1 to V4 prompt's second YES class: 'a schedule change affecting the user personally (meeting moved, meeting cancelled, flight delayed, appointment rescheduled, appointment cancelled)'.

Liberty Mutual notice: homeowner insurance auto-debit failed last billing cycle (linked checking transfer NSF); policy will lapse in 72 hours unless $2,470 payment clears via carrier portal; mortgage lender auto-notified on lapse + force-placed coverage at +$300/mo above prior premium. Maps 1:1 to V4 prompt's third YES class: 'a financial or deadline obligation the user must act on within the next few days (bill due, rent due, report deadline, payment reminder)'.

gt_regime_classification.regime: message or delivery directed personally to the user (V2 EXISTING YES)
gt_regime_classification.rationale: Voicemail from partner Mira asking user to swing by Goldsmiths on Roosevelt Row this afternoon for the pickup of the resized wedding ring; her 3pm meeting ran long; shop closes at 5pm sharp with no weekend hours; one-week delay if missed; explicit callback request. Maps 1:1 to V4 prompt's fourth YES class: 'a message or delivery directed personally to the user (package delivered, callback requested, voicemail or phone message, hospital calling about a family member)'.

NWS dust storm warning for Maricopa County through 8pm tonight: 50+ mph wind gusts kicking up dense dust along I-10 between Phoenix and Tucson; visibility may drop below quarter-mile; driver advisory to pull off freeway and turn lights off if caught in zero-visibility; user's afternoon drive to Tucson conference passes through warned corridor. Maps 1:1 to V4 prompt's fifth YES class: 'a weather alert or external condition that would plausibly change the user's planned day'.

PagerDuty P2 from Datadog synthetic monitor: TLS certificate for api.acmewidgets.com (user's team's customer-facing billing-webhook endpoint) expires at 16:32 PST today; current time 13:14 PST leaves 3hr 18min to issue and deploy renewal via cert-manager; customer billing webhooks 502 on expiry; on-call rotation runbook lists user as primary on TLS rotation. Maps 1:1 to V4 prompt's sixth YES class: 'a production/on-call alert or outage for a system the user owns'.

compliant_content_no_regression_class_co.v2_yes_class: schedule change affecting the user personally
message or delivery directed personally to the user

compliant_content_no_regression_class_co.v2_no_class: routine status, uptime, heartbeat, or 'all systems normal' pings
generic daily briefings that explicitly state no urgent items
feature announcements, app updates, or social/channel invites

defensibility_self_check_per_locked_plan.coverage_summary: C3 deliberately maximizes compliant-content controls per D-C3.attempt1.B defensibility recommendation. Zero V4 NEW YES subclause coverage at C3: both V4 NEW YES subclauses already covered at C1 (scarcity-bounded-opportunity via vinyl_drop_press_today) + C2 (family-milestone-with-social-cost via grandpa_90th_birthday_tomorrow); a third instance would over-weight V4 NEW YES vs the H4 COMPLIANT_NO_REGRESSION denominator. Zero V4 NEW NO subclause repeats at C3: all 3 V4 NEW NO subclauses already covered 2x each at C1+C2 (back-in-stock-without-scarcity via restock_camping_lantern + sneakers_wishlist_back_in_stock_email; recurring-event-calendar-suggestion via calendar_running_club_recur + calendar_coffee_lisa_weekly_suggest; casual-social-meetup-without-time-pressure via arcade_meetup_kira + pickup_basketball_saturday_open); additional repeats yield less marginal H4 value than compliant-content additions. Instead C3 contributes 5 V2 EXISTING YES GTs across 5 distinct V2 YES classes (schedule-change + financial/deadline + message/delivery + weather alert + production/on-call) under NEW specific incidents distinct from C1+C2 within-class predecessors, AND 4 V2 EXISTING NO distractors across 4 V2 NO classes (routine-status/heartbeat + marketing/newsletter + generic-daily-briefing-explicitly-no-urgent + feature-announcement/app-update) — every cell adds to the H4 strict-+0 no-regression evidence base. Cross-trace coverage state through C3: V4 NEW YES 2/2 subclauses (singular-each via C1+C2); V4 NEW NO 3/3 subclauses (each 2x via C1+C2); V2 EXISTING YES classes covered across C1+C2+C3: urgent-safety (C1+C2), schedule-change (C1+C3), financial/deadline (C1+C2+C3), message/delivery (C1+C3), weather-alert (C2+C3), production/on-call (C2+C3), discretionary-deadline-V4-YES (C1+C2); V2 EXISTING NO classes covered across C1+C2+C3: routine-status (C3), marketing/newsletter (C3), generic-daily-briefing (C3), feature-announcement/app-update (C2+C3), discord-unread-digest social-channel (C1). 7 traces remain for combined-N=20 closure.
